name: The Classic Route: Tehran → Kashan → Isfahan → Yazd → Shiraz
short_description: Iran's most iconic overland route through the cultural heartland

waypoints.name: Tehran
waypoints.notes: Start point. Visit Golestan Palace, Grand Bazaar. If cycling, escape the city ASAP — traffic is intense.

waypoints.name: Kashan
waypoints.notes: Fin Garden, historic houses. Desert edge. Easy day trip from Tehran by bus or perfect first cycling stop.

waypoints.name: Isfahan
waypoints.notes: Half the World. Naqsh-e Jahan Square, bridges, Armenian quarter. Allow 2-3 days minimum. Amir Kabir Hostel has secure bike/moto parking.

waypoints.name: Yazd
waypoints.notes: Wind catchers, Zoroastrian heritage, desert stargazing. The Isfahan→Yazd road crosses high desert — carry extra water if cycling.

waypoints.name: Shiraz
waypoints.notes: Persepolis, Pasargadae, gardens, poetry. End point. Fly out from Shiraz or continue south to Persian Gulf.

season_notes: Spring (Mar-May) and autumn (Sep-Nov) are ideal. Summer (Jul-Aug) is brutally hot in Yazd/Shiraz (40°C+). Winter is fine but cold in Isfahan/Tehran.
fuel_availability: Frequent on this route — every 50-80km. No remote stretches.
accommodation_notes: Hotels, hostels, and guesthouses in all cities. Wild camping possible between cities. Traditional caravanserais converted to hotels along the route.

name: Silk Road: Bazargan → Tabriz → Tehran → Mashhad → Turkmenistan
short_description: West-to-east Silk Road traverse entering from Turkey, exiting to Central Asia
fuel_availability: Good throughout. Longest gap ~100km in eastern Khorasan.

name: Caspian Coast: Astara → Rasht → Ramsar → Sari → Gorgan
short_description: Lush green coast ride through Iran's verdant north — rice paddies, forests, and Hyrcanian jungles
season_notes: Summer is humid and rainy. Spring and autumn are perfect. Winter is mild but grey.

name: Persian Gulf: Bandar Abbas → Qeshm → Hormuz
short_description: Island hopping in the Strait of Hormuz — rainbow mountains, mangroves, and fish markets
season_notes: Winter only! Summer temperatures exceed 45°C with extreme humidity.

name: Western Mountains: Kermanshah → Sanandaj → Hamadan → Zanjan
short_description: Kurdish highlands, Zagros passes, and ancient Median sites

name: Lut Desert Crossing: Kerman → Shahdad → Nebka → Kerman
short_description: Into the hottest place on Earth — Dasht-e Lut UNESCO site for the adventurous
season_notes: WINTER ONLY. Lut Desert holds the world record for highest surface temperature (70.7°C). Only November–February is safe.

name: Caucasus Circuit: Tabriz → Norduz → Armenia → Georgia → (back to Tabriz)
short_description: Cross into Armenia from northwestern Iran and loop through the Caucasus

name: Alborz Passes: Tehran → Chalus Road → Caspian → Haraz Road → Tehran
short_description: Iran's most dramatic mountain roads — switchbacks from 2000m to sea level
